title: Complementary health associate professionals
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 39 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Fit patients for orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices, such as crutches, and train patients in their use.
Confer with physical therapy staff or other health care professionals to discuss patient information for treatment planning and coordination.
Assess clients' soft tissue condition, joint quality and function, muscle strength, and range of motion to develop and propose client treatment plans.
Massage and knead muscles and soft tissues of the body to provide treatment for medical conditions, injuries, or wellness maintenance, and perform therapeutic massage techniques.
Provide clients guidance on exercises for postural improvement, stretching, strengthening, relaxation, and rehabilitation.
Monitor operation of equipment, document patient information, and maintain treatment records, ensuring cleanliness and organization of work areas.
Instruct and motivate patients as they practice exercises or functional activities, ensuring they understand techniques and safety.
Administer manual therapeutic exercises and treatments, including performing adjunctive therapies, such as postural drainage and vibrational techniques.
Transport patients to and from treatment areas and assist them in dressing or changing supportive devices as needed.
Attend or conduct continuing education and seminars to remain updated on the field's developments and improve professional skills.
Educate patients regarding treatment plans, physiological reactions to treatment, or post-treatment care.
Monitor patients' food intake and body weight, reporting changes, progress, and dietary problems to dietician.
Plan menus or diets or guide individuals or families in food selection, preparation, or menu planning, based upon nutritional needs and established guidelines.
Analyze menus or recipes, standardize recipes, or test new products.
Conduct physical examinations and physiological function tests for diagnostic purposes.
Monitor patients during tests or surgeries, using electroencephalographs (EEG) or other monitoring equipment.
Perform pulmonary function tests and adjust equipment to obtain optimum results in therapy.
Assist maternal patients to find physical positions that will facilitate childbirth.
Conduct ongoing prenatal health assessments, tracking physical and emotional health changes.
Administer treatments or therapies using physical agents such as air, heat, cold, water, sound, or ultraviolet light to catalyze healing.
Evaluate treatment outcomes and recommend new or altered treatments to promote, restore, or maintain health.
Collaborate in research studies and participate in clinical research projects.
Incorporate research findings into practice and prepare or review reports, manuscripts, or meeting presentations.
Provide information about community health and social resources.
Recommend the use of vitamin and mineral supplements to enhance the health of patients and children and provide patients with contraceptive and family planning information.

Treat patients' symptoms with alternative health care methods such as herbs or hydrotherapy, and evaluate patients' laboratory and medical records, requesting assistance from other practitioners when necessary.
Perform annual gynecologic exams, including pap smears and breast exams, and establish and follow emergency or contingency plans for mothers and newborns.
Prepare charts, graphs, or other visual displays to communicate clients' performance information and document clients' progress toward meeting established treatment objectives.
Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application and analyze data and present findings.
Evaluate, diagnose, or treat disorders, and identify pregnancy-related problems such as hypertension or gestational diabetes.
Study the structure and composition of food or the changes foods undergo in storage and processing.
Develop and implement or evaluate individualized plans for health care, and respond to breech birth presentations by applying appropriate methods.
Analyze determinants responsible for specific inherited traits, and devise methods for altering traits or producing new traits.
Study methods to improve aspects of foods, such as chemical composition, flavor, nutritional value, and convenience, and evaluate food processing operations.
Inspect food processing areas to ensure compliance with government regulations and standards for sanitation, safety, quality, and waste management.
Conduct literature reviews and stay up to date on new regulations regarding food science by reviewing scientific literature.
Prepare plans for management of renewable resources and administer biological research programs.
Study basic principles of plant and animal life, including origin, development, anatomy, and function.
Analyze characteristics of animals to identify and classify them and observe their behavior, ecology, physiology, and nutrition.
Communicate test results and research findings to health practitioners, policy makers, and the public through reports and presentations.
Collect and analyze biological data about relationships among organisms and their environment, including environmental effects on agriculture.
Conduct research in animal nutrition, breeding, and management to improve production processes and product quality.
Study the structure and function of human, animal, and plant tissues, cells, and microorganisms, including their roles in health and disease.
Perform chemical analyses and laboratory procedures to study microorganisms, pollutants, and substances affecting health and growth.
Develop or improve methods for soil management and conservation, including analyses of soil characteristics and plant growth responses.
Provide technical advice to farmers and landowners on sustainable land use practices and ways to improve plant growth.

emerging_tasks.task: Attend high-risk and caesarian section infant deliveries to provide neonatal respiratory care as needed.
Perform general administrative tasks, such as answering telephones, scheduling appointments, or ordering supplies or equipment.
Transport patients to and from the operating room.
Test patients for sexually transmitted infections.
Perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or to obtain information about sources of pollution, contamination, or infection and interpret the results.
Teach epidemiology to students in public health programs.
Test processing equipment to ensure products are produced according to specifications.
Use advanced technologies, such as GIS, remote sensing, and drone technology, for wildlife tracking, habitat mapping, and population studies.
